combined_text:
--- Main Page ---
Dr. Bombay's underwater tea party
The Tea Room
High tea, the loveliest excuse to linger.
SCROLL DOWN
The Tea Room
High tea, the loveliest excuse to linger.
Welcome to Dr. Bombay’s underwater tea party,
When the Ides of March swept through, it carried more than wind—pages turned, stories stirred, and something unseen shifted. Now Hidden behind a weathered door in Grant Park lies a forgotten library, where the story still stirs—weekend high tea, by curious reservation.
Inside a book-lined study, bathed in the ink of a thousand authors, teapots arrive like turning chapters, beside tiered trays of pastries—like secrets meant to be shared. It’s no longer a tea house but a tea room— a place between the lines, where time pauses and the magic never left.
Whether it’s an intimate
high tea for two
, a
celebration for twenty
, or even a
large gathering
, we offer the option to
rent the entire tea room
for special occasions.For bookings and more details, visit our
reservations
page
and secure your spot today!
An impressionistic video set at the iconic Candler Park tea shop, Dr. Bombay's Underwater Tea Party. This neighborhood institution is also host to The Learning Tea, an organization committed to helping impoverished young women in India.
Dr. Bombay's Underwater Tea Party, a community tea house and the cornerstone for our international women's education program
The Learning Tea
. Nestled in Atlanta's historic Grant Park neighborhood, we've been serving up tea, desserts and lasting memories for over a decade.  Shortly after establishing Dr. Bombay's, owner Katrell Christie founded The Learning Tea, which has offered housing, educational and empowerment opportunities for young women in the majestic Indian town of Darjeeling.
The years of great service in Atlanta and overseas have earned Dr. Bombay's and the Learning Tea recognition in publications including Southern Living Magazine, Huffington Post and more.  We are a business happy to serve people of all stripes, colors and creeds.  A large portion of every sale at Dr. Bombay's goes to support the Learning Tea's ongoing mission to improve the lives of its scholars in India.  Whether you're enjoying our signature high tea service, a cup of Darjeeling tea  or one of our delicious desserts, you'll be part of a delightful experience for your senses and a movement that emphasizes giving a hand up instead of a hand out.

--- Page: /menu ---
Dr. Bombay's underwater tea party
High Tea
We serve delicious vegetarian plates, pastries, coffee, and a large selection of teas.
What is High Tea?
Looking for a special experience?
Treat yourself to a
High Tea!
Join us for a delightful afternoon, sipping
hot tea
paired with a delicious assortment of
sandwiches, quiche, cakes, and baked delights
, all elegantly presented on a
vintage china tiered tray
.
Immerse yourself in the
whimsical, eclectic charm
of our inviting tea house, where every detail is designed to create a warm and memorable experience—one that will stay with you long after the last sip.
Reserve your High Tea today!
If you are interested in reserving the entire room for a private party please email us to book the entire space. Your total bill is required upon booking. We do not accept credit card payments by phone.  Cost applies to all  guests including children.
Parking *
We have moved! Dr. Bombay's is located at 753 Cherokee Ave. SE   in Atlanta across from Zoo Atlanta .  Free, on-street parking is available around the Grant Park neighboorhood.
Liability *
You must be at least 21 years of age to sign a reservation agreement.  The person who signs the reservation agreement must be present at the time of the event.  All children under the age of 14 must be accompanied by an adult.  Dr. Bombay's is not responsible for any lost, stolen or damaged personal items.
Please read our complete  Dr. Bombay’s High Tea reservation contract
here
before booking.

meta.meta_description:
Tea house and coffee shop in the Candler Park neighborhood of Atlanta, GA. 
Filled with vintage furniture and decor, this is also a bookshop and home 
of The Learning Tea, a project dedicated to providing scholarships for 
underserved women living in Darjeeling, India.
